disclaimer: All figures are planning assumptions, not investment advice.

phases.label: 0–6 months · Validation & Setup
phases.objective: Stand up Canadian entity, secure local partners, complete regulatory and grid feasibility for 3 candidate sites.
outputs: Signed MOUs with 2 utilities and 1 site host
Compliance review: CSA, Measurement Canada, ZEVIP

phases.label: 6–18 months · Pilot Deployment
phases.objective: Energize 2 commercial DCFC pilot sites and 1 fleet depot with BESS-buffered charging, plus 50–100 home installs through dealer/retail partners.
outputs: 2 DCFC pilot sites operational (4–8 stalls each)
1 fleet depot pilot energized with BESS buffer
Software platform live: telemetry, billing, OCPP 2.0.1
First ZEVIP / NRCan funding application submitted

phases.label: 18–36 months · Expansion
phases.objective: Scale to 15–25 charging sites across ON/QC/BC, deepen utility partnerships, package O&M software for third-party operators.
outputs: Recurring software/O&M revenue from third-party hosts
Battery storage attached to >40% of new sites
Provincial regulator engagement on managed charging tariffs

phases.label: 36+ months · Local Assembly / JV (gated)
phases.objective: Only if pilot economics, policy, and partner trust are validated: stand up Canadian assembly, JV, or selective vehicle import.
outputs: Local assembly of charging hardware and/or BESS modules
JV or strategic minority with Canadian fleet/utility partner
Selective commercial vehicle pilots, only after tariff and trade clarity
Decision gate: continue, pause, or exit

scenarios.label: Conservative
scenarios.notes: Permitting and grid interconnect delays push 30%–50% of sites by 6–12 months. Funding programs partially cover capex.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.notes: Two provinces lead (ON, QC). Recurring O&M and software cover ~30% of revenue by year 5.

scenarios.label: Upside
scenarios.notes: Strong fleet depot pull, BESS attached at >60% of sites, regulated tariff for managed charging unlocks utility co-investment.
